page: Amazon Web Services m8in.xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized virtual machine instance. It is built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and powered by an Intel x86_64 processor. The instance features 4 virtual CPUs (vCPUs) mapped to 2 physical cores with hyper-threading, providing 2 threads per core. The dedicated CPU allocation operates at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz.

The instance is configured with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced memory-to-core ratio of 4.0 GB per physical core. It does not include local storage, meaning all storage operations rely on network-attached Elastic Block Store (EBS) volumes. For network connectivity, the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps. No graphics processing units (GPUs) are included in this configuration, and it does not provide complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m8in.xlarge offers a balanced resource density suitable for workloads requiring a steady ratio of compute power to memory without the overhead of local storage hardware. The dedicated CPU allocation and Nitro hypervisor integration ensure consistent performance. This profile makes the instance suitable for general-purpose workloads, including mid-tier web servers, containerized microservices, enterprise application hosting, and development or testing environments.
description: Amazon Web Services m8in.xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized virtual machine instance powered by an Intel x86_64 processor. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it features 4 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical cores with a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It contains no local storage, relying entirely on network-attached storage, and features a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps. With no integrated GPUs, this instance is designed for workloads requiring a steady balance of compute and memory resources. It is suited for general-purpose applications, including web serving, containerized microservices, and development environments. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent performance for enterprise application hosting.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8in.xlarge is a general-purpose, network-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated Intel vCPUs at 3.9 GHz, 16.0 GB of RAM, and a 6 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.

tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel processors, optimized network performance, and a steady compute-to-memory ratio.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated Intel vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz
16.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
6 Gbps baseline network bandwidth optimized for EBS and network traffic
No local storage, requiring network-attached storage solutions
Suitable for web serving, containerized microservices, and development environments
